paper_title: Sediment and 16S rRNA Gene Sequencing in Moorea, French Polynesia

methods.description: Process for collecting sediment samples from the barrier reef lagoon surrounding the island of Moorea, French Polynesia, for ecological and community analyses.

steps.description: Sediment samples were collected from the barrier reef lagoon surrounding the island of Moorea, French Polynesia.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment samples from Moorea lagoon

steps.description: We collected samples adjacent to the back and fringing reefs within eight replicate 1 m2 plots in and around the Long-Term Ecological Research (LTER) Site 2.
steps.searchable_summary: collect samples from 1 m2 plots near LTER Site 2

steps.description: Within each 1 m2 plot, 16 subsamples were taken using a gridded design.
steps.searchable_summary: take 16 subsamples per plot using gridded design

steps.description: Sediments were sampled every meter along a 4 m transect in each cardinal direction away from the 1m2 plots.
steps.searchable_summary: sample sediments along 4 m transects in cardinal directions

steps.description: All sediments samples (~80 g each) were collected in sterile Whirl-Pak bags and transferred on ice back to the field station for processing.
steps.searchable_summary: store sediment samples in Whirl-Pak bags and transport on ice

steps.description: Approximately 7 g of each sediment sample was transferred to a 15 mL Falcon tube with 7 mL of RNALater for subsequent community analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: transfer sediment to Falcon tube with RNALater

steps.description: All tubes and the remaining sediment were stored at −20°C until processing except when in transit to Scripps Institution of Oceanography when dry ice was used.
steps.searchable_summary: store samples at −20°C or on dry ice during transit

methods.description: Interpolation of LTER data related to N content, heat stress, and coral degradation using geospatial vectors and kriging algorithm.

steps.description: LTER data related to N content, heat stress, and coral degradation were sourced from publicly available data.
steps.searchable_summary: source LTER data on N content, heat stress, coral degradation

steps.description: In R, public geospatial data from LTER monitoring was interpolated by converting geographic coordinates for our collection sites to geospatial vectors.
steps.searchable_summary: convert geographic coordinates to geospatial vectors in R

steps.description: Applying a kriging algorithm to predict environmental values at our sites.
steps.searchable_summary: apply kriging algorithm to predict environmental values

methods.description: DNA extraction and sequencing of sediment samples for 16S rRNA gene analysis to study microbial communities.

steps.description: All quadrat samples (n = 128) and transect tips (n = 32) were processed for DNA extraction.
steps.searchable_summary: process quadrat and transect samples for DNA extraction

steps.description: Mid-point transect samples (n = 4) were also processed for site 3, which was qualitatively identified as having the highest coral cover.
steps.searchable_summary: process mid-point transect samples for site 3

steps.description: All remaining transect samples from site 6 were also processed to increase spatial comparisons among the algae-dominated site.
steps.searchable_summary: process remaining transect samples from site 6

steps.description: For DNA extraction, ~2 g of sediment in RNALater was processed by the Center for Microbiome Innovation (CMI) at UC San Diego using the MagAttract PowerSoil DNA KF kit (Qiagen).
steps.searchable_summary: extract DNA using MagAttract PowerSoil DNA KF kit

steps.description: Samples were subsequently sequenced with the MiSeq System PE150 platform (Illumina) using the V4 region 515 forward primer and the updated 806 reverse primer.
steps.searchable_summary: sequence DNA using MiSeq System with V4 region primers

steps.description: For increased sequencing depth to capture the rare biosphere, two separate sequencing runs were performed for community analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: perform two sequencing runs for increased depth
